_meta.author: va-legislature-refine (overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize VA House of Delegates batch HD52-56: Wendell Walker (R-52), Tim Griffin (R-53), Katrina Callsen (D-54), Amy Laufer (D-55), Tom Garrett (R-56). Cited research per legislator; every TRUE/FALSE carries a source URL; unsupported applicable cells reset to null. Cross-party deviation captured: Tom Garrett (R) voted YEA on HJ9 (same-sex-marriage amendment) -> biblical_marriage scored FALSE.

profile.religion: Baptist — member, Thomas Road Baptist Church (Lynchburg)
profile.background: B.A. pastoral ministry, Liberty University (1984); former landscaping-business owner; ~20yr workforce-development career with the Commonwealth; Chairman, Lynchburg Republican City Committee. First elected 2019 (then HD-23; HD-52 after 2021 redistricting).

wendell-walker.notes_set: Evidence_state finalization 2026-06-02. Pro-life sponsor: chief patron HB1395 (2022, right to life begins at conception) and born-alive bills (HB1795 2023); voted NO on the 2025 reproductive-freedom amendments (HJR1/SJR247). Voted NO on HJ9 (2025) repealing the man-woman marriage definition. Voted NO on HB2 (2024) assault-weapons ban. Chief patron HB1572 (2023) swatting-penalty / pro-law-enforcement bill. No public record surfaced on christian-liberty, election-integrity, federal-overreach, immigration, or most family/economic dimensions — those left null (not inferred). Lone off-pattern item: 2020 ERA YES vote, which he publicly said he intended as nay (not scored).

0.note: Chief patron HB1395 (2022): right to life begins at conception / personhood from conception.

1.note: Patroned HB1395 (personhood) and born-alive infant-protection bills (HB1795 2023); voted NO on the 2025 reproductive-freedom amendments. Personhood/abolitionist framing, not merely a gestational restriction.

2.note: No public position found on embryonic/IVF/chimeric research.

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: VPAP donor data inaccessible this session; no evidence either way on PP/NARAL/EMILY's List funding.

0.note: Voted NAY on HJ9 (2025), the amendment repealing Virginia's one-man/one-woman marriage provision — i.e., voted to keep the man-woman definition.

1.note: Same NAY on HJ9 opposes constitutionalizing same-sex marriage.

2.note: No specific transgender/biological-sex bill or vote found.

3.note: No record on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: No specific vote on LGBTQ+ promotion in schools/policy.

0.note: Generic campaign 'parental choice in education' language only; no recorded school-choice/homeschool vote or bill.

1.note: No record on parental notification for gender interventions.

0.note: No specific bill/vote.

1.note: No specific bill/vote.

0.note: No CBDC/cash position found.
No record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: Chief patron HB1572 (2023) strengthening swatting/false-threat penalties, said inspired by local law enforcement; public community-policing support — affirmative pro-LE advocacy.

1.note: No specific vote on cashless bail / rogue prosecutors.

0.note: No 10A/nullification record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: No record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: NRA member / generic 2A campaign language; no constitutional-carry vote found.

1.note: Voted NAY on HB2 (2024) assault-weapons ban; quoted opposing the ban as turning law-abiding citizens into criminals.

profile.religion: Christian (per official LIS member profile; no denomination/church or pastoral role publicly documented — he is an attorney, NOT a pastor)
profile.background: Attorney (constitutional/election law); former Assistant Commonwealth's Attorney. Liberty University (B.A. 2007), Appalachian School of Law (J.D. 2011); clerked for VA Supreme Court Justice Kinser and U.S. District Judge Turk. Former Chairman, Bedford County Republican Committee. Member since 2024.

tim-griffin.notes_set: Evidence_state finalization 2026-06-02. CORRECTION: prior 'pastor' descriptor unsupported — LIS lists occupation Attorney, religion Christian. Voted NAY on all stages of the reproductive-freedom (HJ1/SJ1) and marriage (HJ9/HJ3/SJ3) constitutional amendments; chief patron of HB2405 (2025, ban minor gender-reassignment surgery) and HB158 (2026, child gender-transition = child abuse); chief patron HJ73 (2026, Christian Heritage Week). Pro-LE legislation enacted (HB1874 retired-LEO info protection; HB1815 LODA campus police; HB1734 sex-trafficking coordinator). Uniform NAY on the 2025-26 gun-control slate (AWB, red-flag, waiting periods). Voted NAY on bills limiting local-federal immigration cooperation (HB1441/SB783 2026). Endorsed by Virginia Society for Human Life. No CBDC/balanced-budget/usury/ESG, federal-nullification, or border-barrier/deportation/E-Verify record found — left null.

0.note: On the floor tied abortion to 'the violence the Democrat constitutional amendment will do to unborn babies' — affirms unborn personhood; endorsed by Virginia Society for Human Life.

1.note: Consistent NAY on the repro-freedom amendment (defensive) but no affirmative abortion-abolition bill sponsored; held to the high 'abolition not merely restriction' bar -> null.

4.note: No donor data confirming PP/NARAL/EMILY's List funding either way.

0.note: Voted NAY on the marriage amendment at every stage (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026, SJ3 2026) — voted to keep the man-woman definition.

1.note: Same NAY votes oppose constitutionalizing same-sex marriage.

2.note: Chief patron HB2405 (2025, ban gender-reassignment surgery on minors) and HB158 (2026, defining child gender-transition as child abuse) — affirms biological sex against transgender ideology.

4.note: Patroning HB158/HB2405 opposes facilitation of gender-ideology in public policy affecting minors; campaign also opposes 'trans ideology' in schools (soft).

0.note: Campaign 'we must pass school choice' (soft) but no school-choice bill reached a floor vote under the Democratic majority -> null.

1.note: Chief patron HB158 protecting minors from gender-transition interventions — aligns with parental consent/notification on gender-related interventions for minors.

2.note: Campaign opposition to CRT/trans ideology is soft; no standalone curriculum vote found -> null.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB778 (2026), 'Display of obscene material to a minor unlawful; penalty' (96-0) — criminal penalties for sexualized content reaching minors.

0.note: No specific profess-Christ bill/vote.

1.note: No conscience-exemption bill found.

3.note: Chief patron HJ73 (2026), designating Christian Heritage Week — public-square recognition of Christian heritage.

0.note: No CBDC/cash position found (tax-cut bills HJ14/HJ15/HB703 do not map to this cell).
No paper-ballot record.

1.note: Campaign supports photo ID (soft); no GOP voter-ID bill reached a floor vote -> null.

3.note: Consistent NAY on Democratic mail-in/absentee expansion (absentee-deadline extensions HB82/SB58 2026, satellite-voting offices HB2056 2025, Sunday early voting SB438 2026) — opposes expansion of mass mail-in/early voting. (Member-vote record, MemberID H0364.)

0.note: Chief patron of pro-law-enforcement bills enacted in 2025: HB1874 (protect retired-LEO personal info) and HB1815 (Line of Duty Act for campus/private police).

1.note: Voted NAY on HB2328 (2025) expanding bail release for pregnant/recently-pregnant persons — opposed easing pretrial detention.

2.note: Chief patron HB1734 (sex-trafficking response coordinator) and HB1781 (enforcement of foreign protective orders in family-abuse cases) — victim protection.

0.note: No 10A/nullification record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: No border-barrier record (state legislator).

1.note: No deportation record.

2.note: Voted NAY on HB1441/SB783 (2026), which would restrict local law-enforcement cooperation with federal immigration authority — i.e., opposed sanctuary-style limits on ICE cooperation.

0.note: Campaign 2A language (soft); no constitutional-carry bill reached a floor vote -> null.

1.note: Uniform NAY on the 2025-26 gun-control slate: assault-weapons bans (HB1607 2025, HB217 2026), red-flag-order expansion (SB495 2026), waiting periods (HB2631 2026). (Member-vote record, MemberID H0364.)

profile.background: Attorney; former Deputy City Attorney, City of Charlottesville; former Chair, Albemarle County School Board (Rio District); Teach for America alum. B.A. Yale (2008), J.D. University of Virginia (2014). Member since 2024; re-elected unopposed 2025.

katrina-callsen.notes_set: Evidence_state finalization 2026-06-02. Progressive Democrat; documented contrary positions to the rubric: co-patron + YEA on HJ1 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment) and HJ9 (2025 marriage-equality amendment); received $5,000 from EMILY's List; campaign platform backs gender-affirming care for youth, conversion-therapy ban, no-excuse absentee voting; chief patron HB1876 (ban firearms in Commonwealth buildings) and co-patron of the assault-weapons ban (HB1607) and secure-storage mandate (HB1597); bail bills easing pretrial detention (HB1873). No documented positions aligning with the rubric were found (no pro-LE advocacy, no conservative cross-votes), so aligned cells are null and contrary cells FALSE — evidence-honest, not party-inferred. Religion not publicly documented (left unset).

0.note: Co-patron and YEA on HJ1 (2025), the constitutional amendment enshrining a fundamental right to reproductive freedom incl. abortion — contrary to life-from-conception.

1.note: Same YEA + co-patron of the abortion-rights amendment is the documented contrary of abortion abolition.

2.note: No specific position on embryonic research/IVF discard.

0.note: Co-patron and YEA on HJ9 (2025), repealing the man-woman marriage provision — contrary to affirming man-woman marriage.

1.note: Same YEA constitutionalizes same-sex marriage.

2.note: Platform: 'safeguard gender-affirming healthcare' and protect LGBTQ+ youth — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Platform: ban conversion therapy, 'ensure everyone feels welcome in our schools,' protect LGBTQ+ youth — supports LGBTQ+ promotion in policy/schools.

0.note: Public-school-funding focus; no recorded vote opposing school choice -> null.

1.note: Stated support for gender-affirming healthcare for youth — contrary to parental notification/consent on gender interventions for minors.

2.note: No specific recorded vote on CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curricula -> null.

0.note: No specific record.

1.note: No specific conscience-exemption record (the clergy-refusal clause in HJ9 is incidental, not her advocacy).

0.note: No CBDC/cash record.
No paper-ballot record.

1.note: No specific photo-voter-ID vote -> null.

3.note: Platform supports no-excuse absentee voting, same-day registration, and early voting; opposes 'voter suppression' — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting.

0.note: No defund vote and no pro-LE advocacy found -> null.

1.note: Chief patron HB1873 (2025, release of accused on secured/unsecured bond) and decarceral platform (end mandatory minimums, expand diversion) — contrary to opposing soft-on-crime/cashless-bail policy.

0.note: No 10A/nullification record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: No record.

1.note: No record.

0.note: Chief patron HB1876 (2025) banning firearms in Commonwealth-owned/leased buildings and Capitol Square — contrary to constitutional carry.

1.note: Co-patron HB1607 (assault-weapons ban) and HB1597 (secure-storage mandate); platform endorses red-flag laws and universal background checks.

profile.background: Former public-school science teacher and state water chemist; Charlottesville City School Board 2012-2019 (chair/vice-chair 2015-16); founder of Virginia's List (2014), a PAC to elect pro-choice Democratic women. B.S. geology (UW-Milwaukee); M.A. science education (Columbia Teachers College); Peace Corps (Jamaica). Member since 2024; re-elected 2025.

amy-laufer.notes_set: Evidence_state finalization 2026-06-02. Democrat; documented contrary positions to the rubric: founded Virginia's List (pro-choice PAC) and campaigns to 'codify reproductive freedom into the Virginia Constitution'; supported the marriage-equality amendment and campaigns to 'remove the Marshall-Newman Amendment'; former Moms Demand Action volunteer, patron of HB110 (2026, unattended-firearm civil penalty), platform backs red-flag laws and firearm registration. Record is thinner than her HD-54 colleague (fewer culture-war patronages), so most cells are null (no evidence) and only documented-contrary cells are FALSE. ReproRising VA graded her A+ (soft). Religion not reliably sourced (left unset).

0.note: Founded Virginia's List (pro-choice org); campaign platform: 'codify Roe / codify reproductive freedom into the Virginia Constitution' — contrary to life-from-conception.

1.note: Pro-choice advocacy and support for the reproductive-freedom amendment is the documented contrary of abortion abolition.

2.note: No specific position on embryonic research/IVF discard.

4.note: Founded Virginia's List, a PAC funding pro-choice candidates — deeply tied to the abortion-rights funding network (stronger than merely accepting such funding).

0.note: Campaign: 'remove the Marshall-Newman Amendment'; supported HJ9 (all voting Democrats voted yes) — contrary to affirming man-woman marriage.

1.note: Same marriage-equality advocacy constitutionalizes same-sex marriage.

profile.background: U.S. Army officer (Field Artillery, Captain, 1995-2000); former Louisa County Commonwealth's Attorney and Assistant Attorney General; former VA State Senator (22nd Dist., 2012-17) and U.S. Congressman (VA-05, 2017-19, House Freedom Caucus). University of Richmond (B.A., J.D.). Won HD-56 in 2023; member since 2024.

tom-garrett.notes_set: Evidence_state finalization 2026-06-02. Conservative Republican with one documented cross-party deviation: voted YEA on HJ9 (2025) repealing the man-woman marriage provision, explaining 'I'm defending people's right to be free' (biblical_marriage scored FALSE accordingly). Otherwise: NAY on HJ1 (repro amendment), '100% pro-life / no exceptions' pledge; chief patron HB2073 (anti-debanking with explicit anti-ESG and religious-institution conscience carve-outs) and HB984 (cash-payment protection); HB993 (2026, discontinue driver privilege cards for undocumented immigrants); HB981 (prosecutorial-misconduct accountability) and HB1968 (crime-victim compensation); repeated floor speeches against the 2024/2026 gun-control packages; endorsed by NRA/VCDL/Gun Owners of America. Religion invoked rhetorically ('the Creator') but no denomination publicly documented (left unset). Past U.S. House controversies noted but not scored.

0.note: Voted NAY on HJ1 (2025 repro-freedom amendment); '100% pro-life' and pledge to 'support legislation that recognizes life begins at conception.'

1.note: On life, pledged to 'protect the first and foremost inalienable right ... No exceptions' — abolitionist (no-exceptions) framing.

4.note: VPAP donor data inaccessible; no evidence either way.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YEA on HJ9 (2025) repealing the man-woman marriage definition, explaining 'I reconcile my faith with my vote in saying I'm defending people's right to be free.'

1.note: Same YEA on HJ9 constitutionalizes same-sex marriage.

0.note: HB990 (higher-ed scholarship fund) is not a clean K-12 school-choice/homeschool measure -> null.

1.note: No parental-notification-on-gender record.

3.note: Child-sexual-abuse-penalty bills (HB2075/HB989) are not a porn-age-verification measure -> null.

0.note: No profess-Christ record.

1.note: Chief patron HB2073 (2025), whose anti-debanking text includes an explicit carve-out protecting religious institutions' right to make service determinations based on religious belief — conscience protection.

0.note: Chief patron HB984 (2026), 'cash payments, discrimination prohibited' — protects cash as accepted tender, aligned with the pro-cash / anti-cashless-control position.

4.note: Chief patron HB2073 bars financial institutions from discriminating based on failure to meet ESG / corporate-diversity standards — explicit anti-ESG measure.

0.note: No paper-ballot record.

1.note: No voter-ID record.

2.note: HJ50 (study of 45-day early voting) is a study, not a position -> null.

4.note: HB982 (deepfake campaign-ad regulation) does not map to Zuckerbucks/foreign-funding -> null.

0.note: No defund vote / pro-LE advocacy bill found (former Commonwealth's Attorney is background, not a scoreable position) -> null.

1.note: Chief patron HB981 (2026), 'prosecutorial misconduct; reasonable costs and attorney fees' — accountability for rogue prosecutors.

2.note: Chief patron HB1968 (2025, crime-victim compensation, extends filing time); publicly conditioned felon re-enfranchisement on victim restitution first — victim-centered justice.

0.note: No state-level 10A/nullification vote (Freedom Caucus membership was federal-era).

1.note: No record.

2.note: Gun floor-rhetoric is general, not a state-vs-federal resistance vote -> null.

0.note: No border-barrier record.

1.note: No deportation record.

2.note: Chief patron HB993 (2026) discontinuing driver privilege cards for undocumented immigrants — opposes a state accommodation of illegal immigration (anti-sanctuary-spirit).

0.note: Campaign '100% pro-gun, supports constitutional carry'; sole HD-56 candidate endorsed by NRA, VCDL, and Gun Owners of America.

1.note: Repeated documented floor speeches opposing the 2024 and 2026 gun-control packages (assault-weapons/magazine bans, red-flag expansion).
